title: Lucidna
body: DNA inside out
Projects include 4th generation DNA sequencing, Storegg human ovum cryo-storage, biobanks, and DNA-based census of all (8 billion) humans.

In 4th generation DNA sequencing, Lucidna is engineering DNA polymerase to work as a nanopore. Combining DNA polymerase with a signaling pathway should generate a base-specific signal for each nucelotide. The signaling pathway may be wavelength-dependent luciferase, fluorescence, or quantum dots.

Storegg is to save humanity from the birth rate crisis. We believe any humans who want to have children should be able to do so, so we're increasing the fertile window for human females through cryo-storage. While ovum storage is not new, it is still very expensive. We're collaborating with egg storage facilities and bearing the initial and yearly costs of ovum storage for 10 years. This investment (funded in part by government subsidies) is a bet on the future. Starting in Italy and Japan.

Biobanks store human cells for future use. Stem cells stored in biobanks may be used in for organ regeneration, and personalized medicine that lowers immune response. We understand the importance of biobanks and hypothesize most medicines of the future will be personalized. By linking biobanks to ASBL, we intend to manufacture better medicines.

DNA-based humanity census ascertains verifiable humanity. Human life is most valuable is the tenet The Matrix is built upon. We realiably ascertain humans in our system to use the entire system to assist the humans. Faces change, fingerprints erode, eyes get damaged, but DNA remains mostly constant and trackable through blood tests. This is why identity in The Matrix needs a DNA-based humanity census. A ledger of all humanity. Used for revive ledger.

We won't dnabank people who choose no outreach (uncontacted peoples), though they are forever welcome. Visas, passports, and social security numbers need this update; how long will humanity go through stamps on paper and address-based identity? This is a human verification layer. Lucidna prioritizes privacy because DNA sequence data is the best identifier of humans. Since DNA is easily accessible (humans have hairfall, dead skin drops off), a version of DNA needs to be made that is based on an individual's DNA yet extremely difficult to generate unless you are truly that human. We're working on it.

About
Matrixnet is a retreat into the original vision of the Internet, now referred to as Web 1.0. Matrixnet destroys the idea of large corporations controlling the Internet. Before 2025, large swathes of the Internet were controlled by large corporations like Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft. These corporations relied on advertisements to generate revenue. They made the Internet a cesspool of spam ads, data theft, and made privacy a joke. Matrixnet rebukes the Internet of 2024. Richard Hendricks from Silicon Valley (HBO show) envisioned something similar to Matrixnet.

Matrixnet Bitcoin Bank
Matrixnet is ad-free and generates revenue from commissions on payments powered by its Bitcoin-based bank, Matrixnet Bitcoin Bank (Matrixbank). Each Matrixnet user has a digital bank account to store Bitcoin. Matrixbank guarantees 1 BTC for each user annually. A user can pay up to 10 million finney to other users on credit from Matrixbank every year. Each payment of n finney using Matrixbank carries a note: Matrixnet Bitcoin Bank guarantees the receiver of this transaction n finney. Matrixbank gives liquidity to holders of Bitcoin with its trusted payment network, enabled by controlled access. Controlled access is Matrixbank's KYC (know-your-customer).

Botnet
Botnet is a browser for Matrixnet. Botnet accesses a network of bots like browsers access the Internet, a network of websites. Each bot in Botnet has a chatbot interface. Matrixnet users build API backends for services provided by companies such as travel booking, web hosting, and more. A service bot accesses relevant API backends to perform tasks. Each bot in Botnet takes text and GUI input.

Botnet plans for apps, websites, and other media to be generated and rendered natively in a device-independent manner. Device independence is achieved through a new CPU architecture developing in CNS Robots.

For example, Expedia's chatbot books flight tickets from text input (book flight from Delhi to London on 25 January). Botnet accesses your data from Personal Databank to fill information like your name, passport number, visa validity, phone number, email, and payment details. When you give consent to book a ticket, Botnet accesses your data on PDB for a limited period of time, gives data to Expedia's bot which books the ticket, gives your payment information to Stripe's bot, and name, email, and travel itinerary to United Airlines' bot. These bots exist in a society of bots called Botnet, and work together to book your ticket with minimal manual input from you.

Build these bots for Botnet:
Tufts bot prints IDs, reserves rooms, does payroll, gets college news, and applies to on-campus jobs, all in a clean text format.
News bot delivers news to you, powered by a public ranked forum where truth is verified using votes. Think ETH staking but with credibility for news sources (humans, news channels, Big News). Trust rankings determine what news gets to you.
Flight bot books cheap flights in text. No need to navigate different websites and find the best price, let it book tickets and send them to you.
Jobs bot connects to universities to verify degrees, connects to Research Engine to verify letters of recommendation, and autofills job applications based on skill relevance.

Controlled access
The crux of Matrixnet is controlled access. Getting access to Matrixnet is a difficult task. Only authorized humans can give new users access to Matrixnet. Currently, only the founder Noorul Ali authorizes new users. Controlled access powers all Matrixnet apps like Bitcoin bank, Matrixnet Email, Botnet, and Dexnet. Controlled access is the key behind spam elimination that makes Matrixnet a clean new Internet.

Dexnet
Dexnet is a collection of dexs built by dexweb from Research Engine. Each dex is a programmatically generated text-only website. Dexnet collects dexs in a public list for people read freely. Dexnet contains research journals, company financial reports, personal journals, and more.

Matrixnet Email
Matrixnet eliminates spam using 3-way handshakes ensuring people have the right to revoke access to them on Matrixnet. This is to be implemented first in Matrixnet Email. An identification system generates tokens necessary for access to inbox, inspired by cookie-based identification. Spam is eliminated by revoking access to tokens.
New users get a Matrixbank bank account, their dex hosted on Dexnet, and an email account in Matrixnet Email. They are officially members of The Matrix.

Personal Databank
Personal Databank (PDB) is a device to store all your data in your home. Time-scrambled data are released from PDB based on wilful consent. PDB is a device sold as an appliance. It connects to all state machines in a spatial box (physical house). It backs up all data. PDB is linked to every laptop, phone, TV, fridge, autokitchen, IoT device, garage door, and appliance in a household. PDB stores all kinds of data for each unique individual in the house, in a contaminant-free manner (users can only access and release their data, no central control over all data). It lets out time-scrambled data to services only till they need them. For example, Doordash and Amazon only need location when an order is placed. Controlled data release based on user preference enforces consent and privacy.

A PDB usecase is The Matrix Feed which collates some data into a webpage showing what logged in users in The Matrix are doing. Trading on Antarctic Stock Simulation, building worlds in Matrixims, creating life using ASBL machines, reading dexs on Dexnet or building bots on Botnet.

Data can be processed to find mates for people, like personal Tinder. Dystopian we know, but why must it be so? You are encouraged to meet people in real life. You simply have a choice to take advantage of large-scale statistics to help you choose a mate. Reiterating choice: you are free to not partake in data-based love search. That is the point of Personal Databank. You control where your data are used.

Each PDB is yours. Owned by you. Truly and completely owned by you. Tear it up, break it apart, or re-engineer it entirely. You might get a call of The Matrix if you make it better. Matrixnet owns nothing of a Personal Databank it has already sold, including its software. PDB software is owned by users and not simply licensed like most software. You can modify it. Matrixnet provides services so databanks can operate and we incorporate that in our (high) price. But the databank and all data in it are yours. We have no right, no access over it, and this is verified by others (Algobank). If you choose to give us some of your data, we'll incorporate it in different parts of The Matrix like Matrixims, The Matrix Feed, and Lucidna.

Apps
Matrixnet applications have programmatic access. Let us explain with the example of this website (The Matrix Dex). The Matrix Dex is a Matrixnet application. While you can view this website in a browser by opening index.html (GUI view), you can also edit a version of this website on your computer with an editor like VSCode (Matrixnet Programmatic Access or MPA). You can search, index and analyze this website using Python using the raw JSON source of this website. This website has Matrixnet Programmatic Access enabled.

Programmatic access is an integral feature of Matrixnet applications. Spotify on Matrixnet will allow a user to get a JSON list of all their Liked Songs using a programming language and MPA. Each Matrixnet app can be used by GUI and programs through MPA. Matrixnet Programmatic Access (MPA) simplifies building applications on top of applications, like MCP (Model Context Protocol) and API (Application Programming Interface).

title: CNS Robots
body: Robots with a central nervous system

Robots have limbs, wheels, and actuators. These devices run on numbers. A sequence of bits programs a motor to spin at some frequency, and limbs to have a specific configuration. This is very similar to how human limbs work. Electric and chemical signals cause limbs to behave in a certain way. There is information equivalence between the two. CNS robots will have no firmware, driver, or compiler layers. Layers of abstraction from electric currents (bits) to Python programs were designed for human beings, because we cannot think in bits. Neural networks don’t have this limitation. They can think in numbers (artificial) or electrical and chemical signals (biological). A neural network can thus learn behavior without needing explicit programming. This is the next step in robotics. CNS robots that learn behavior through numbers or information. Like how a human baby learns.

Builds robots with a central nervous system, using information mechanics (IM). New generation of botmaking. Layered software architecture was designed to split development among humans. IM makes building robots akin to behavior learning like in living organisms. Searching for rapid automated development.

Rentalife was born as Lazrlake in Mumbai, India in 2022. Founder Noorul Ali's apartment rent was higher than its mortgage. Inaccessibility and the rigid nature of mortgages deter youth from homeownership, forcing them to rent. Lazrlake was designed to offer 30-year mortgage into the idea of a home instead of a physical home. A tenant pays monthly for 30 years to purchase an abstract home recorded digitally. In exchange, Lazrlake planned to offer housing units around the world.

Rentalife upgrades Lazrlake with one monthly payment for a car, home, groceries and cleaning services. Rentalife offers a monthly subscription to a lifestyle that includes a house, car, groceries and cleaning services. Rentalife is the hotel life by month. All utilities paid for, locations cleaned weekly and furnished homes.
Features
Rentalife units are not standardized; each has a unique design, architecture and aesthetic
Always fully furnished

Hightech housing. Rentalife units are experimental testbeds for other projects in The Matrix. Units may include automated floor cleaning vents, butler automata, autokitchen, blue collar bots, purified air bubble enclosure, bioengineered plants and other technologies.

Fractional ownership encouraged. If a user stays only 5 years in one Rentalife unit, they paid 4 years mortgage and own a percentage of the unit. Subsequent rental or mortgage of this unit generates financial return for the fractional owner.

Guardrails on residences as investment. While fractional ownership is encouraged, a unit cannot be rented forever by the same tenant. Mortgage automatically starts if a user stays for 13 months. Rental payment to fractional owner decreases over time because rent payment decreases fractional ownership.
What the rich have assistants for, Rentalife includes it in monthly rent. Monthly payments, rent, insurance, utility bills, other bills: Rentalife takes care of it all.
Flexibility
No security deposit
No multiple month rent upfront
Monthly rental agreements only
Pay, stay, extend or leave by 1st of next month
No lock-in ever
Move into another Rentalife unit anywhere in the world or just move out
Homeownership

Continuous residence in one Rentalife unit for 12 months unlocks mortgage. Rent 13th month onwards is counted as mortgage. Each rent payment 13th month onwards in the same unit increases residing Rentalife user's ownership in the unit.

If the Rentalife user moves of a Rentalife unit they have ownership in, a portion of the Rentalife monthly payment paid for the unit by a new Rentalife user is paid to the unit owner, even with fractional ownership. Payment of a portion of the monthly payment to the unit owner decreases their fractional ownership over time. If the Rentalife unit is mortgaged, the entire Rentalife monthly payment is used to purchase ownership of the previous owner first. Once ownership of previous owner ends, Rentalife is paid the Rentalife monthly payment to increase ownership of the current Rentalife user residing in the unit.

Rentalife is designed for flexible individuals without a permanent address. These Rentalife power users own an idea of a home, not a physical home. This idea of a home (Rentalife membership) lets Rentalife users access housing across the world. Rentalife will have units everywhere: Antarctica, Moon, Mars, deep space and other galaxies.
Technology
Clean air, robots, automation and more in development

2. Nizdevco | Delhi, India | 8988 units available

Designed for students, startups and hackers, Engineer Pad is inspired from Noorul Ali and roommates' residence in graduate school at Tufts University. 114 Harvard St, Medford, housed 4 Tufts graduate engineering students who contributed to Rentalife's technology. Anand Pradeep Kumar Sharma ideated automated floor cleaning vents and autokitchen. Pushkar ideated car, groceries and cleaning packaged in one rental payment, and spaceship houses. Amoldip Singh Narang ideated butler automata. Noorul ideated flexible homeownership with rent (Lazrlake).

Engineer Pad encourages customization. Residents can develop technology for use in Rentalife units. Rentalife will license useful technology developed by Rentalife users like residents of Engineer Pad.

Nizamuddin Basti Slum Redevelopment Company (NIZDEVCO) is a Rentalife project in New Delhi, India to redevelop Hazrat Nizamuddin Basti, a neigborhood in South Delhi, into a walkable, green and climate resilient tourist destination. Nizamuddin is home to world-famous tourist destinations like Hazrat Nizamuddin Dargah, the tomb of 13th century Sufi saint Nizamuddin Auliya; Banglewali Masjid Nizamuddin, a large mosque attracting pilgrims from across the world; and Pracheen Shiv Shakti Mandir, a historic 11th century temple and the focal point of settlement in the neighborhood. Nizdevco is aiming to redevelop the area of Hazrat Nizamuddin Basti into a walkable car-free tourist destination.

NRN is an identity standing tall amidst design-starved Delhi NCR architecture. Ugly shops and malls with haphazard signs, unpleasant colors, loud ads and peeling paint are ugly modern architecture in Delhi. NRN is a design masterpiece of a town existing for aesthetic viewing pleasure. A designed city to inspire design in Delhi.

NRN is an iconic town designed to stand out when crossed in Delhi Metro. When aboveground Metro crosses NRN centre, beautiful buildings, parks and tech demos stand out as people view a city of the future. Other Metro lines crossing NRN from outside see a scenic view of modern skyscrapers, skypiercers and protected environments. NRN environment is protected from nature with engineering (Restorever).

3 residential skyscrapers are planned in phase 1. Skyscrapers 1 and 2 house engineers of The Matrix. Skyscraper 3 houses NRN service providers like cleaners, cooks and shopkeepers. NRN is a vertical, technology centered town with 24x7 clean filtered air using Restorever technology.
Skyscrapers 1 and 2 have 1 apartment per floor. 25 units reserved.

Article: Death of representative democracy
by Noorul Ali, leader of The Matrix Government
Motivation: Failure of representative democracy, Earth 2025

Representative democracy failed on Earth in 2025. This failure became evident in the international support of Israel's genocide against Palestine citizens. Democratic governments across USA, Italy, United Kingdom, Canada, France and other countries support Israel with weapons exports. These governments are complicit is this horrific genocide. Yet, citizens of these nations are against weapons exports to Israel. Above 51% of eligible voters are likely against weapons exports to Israel, because USA has $34 trillion in national debt (120% of GDP). Weapons exports to Israel are foreign aid and a major expense on USA and other democracies. National debt exceeding GDP is mirrored in other Western democracies as well. Yet, representative government leaders in US Congress, many of whom receive donations from a pro-Israel lobbying group called AIPAC, pass legislation for aid to Israel in the form of weapons exports. This is failure of representative democracy. Representatives failed to act in the interest of who they represent.

In the Internet age, representative democracy is no longer needed. Representative democracy was needed because millions of citizens could not directly vote on legislation. They would vote once every few years, electing representatives who would vote on behalf of the citizens who elected them. In today's age, billions of people use Internet applications. We can ascertain digital identity with identity verification platforms like Clear. We are able to ascertain human identity online and build platforms to support billions of users.

The Matrix Government is built on the idea of Direct Democracy. People directly vote on legislation in Direct Democracy. Voting technology is electronic, transparent, open-source and independently verifiable. Should we gift $10 billion of arms to Israel? 300 million Americans should vote on it, not 535 senators.

The Matrix Government is a new idea of government. Citizens embedded in the very fabric of government without middlemen. Direct Democracy is the soul of The Matrix Government. Direct Democracy may be superseded in turbulent times by a single authority figure. This is inspired by republic because there are times when one person must decide the future of a nation.

ZEROZENO was ideated in 2022 as ZEROZENO NFT collection to help students. ZEROZENO helped students mint NFTs (non-fungible tokens) to pay for their education. 80% of an NFT's sale price was transferred to the student minter. 20% was taken as fee to fund higher education of ZEROZENO founder Noorul Ali. This NFT collection never came to be but highlights the founding principle of ZEROZENO: give youth money to achieve dreams.

ZEROZENO Venture Capital takes shares in the works of youth capitalists. The firm funds founders distorting reality by the force of sheer will. Visions so powerful, founders so stubbon, the world has no choice but to change. Founder minds hellbent on moulding the world in their images. I am honored to know these beautiful minds. Minds who shape humanity's destiny and chart humankind's future through the next 100 years.
The World Is Yours
Why the name ZEROZENO?

Startups seem to be zero from the outside for a long time (ZERO). Suddenly, they jump and reach one (ONE, reverse of ENO). This inflection point from 0 to 1, this unpredictable path (Z E R O then O N E), irrational against all odds, this differentiates startup from business. A startup is expected to fail. A startup is expected to stay at zero. ZEROZENO captures a startup defying all odds and reaching one with an unexpected jump. The startup reaches O of ENO and charts a reverse (unpredictable) path to ONE. This jump starts up the startup. This jump is what a startup lives for.
ZEROZENO is also named for Zeno's Dichotomy paradox. The reader is left to wonder why.

Exponential growth also differentiates startup from business. Somewhere in the growth of a startup, exponential growth must occur. Maybe in user growth, when existing users overwhelmingly recommend or attract more users. Maybe in revenue growth, when small initial sales snowball into bigger and bigger sales, like addictive apps. Maybe in network effects, where your product is the backbone of new applications. A startup must taste that sweet addictive nectar of exponential growth to be more than just a business.

Noorul Ali (GP) runs 3 startups (ASBL, ONITO and Restorever) and one non-profit (Research Engine).

ZEROZENO will not ever invest in startups with Noorul Ali's ownership in personal capacity, like the startups above. This is to prevent conflicts of interest.

title: Mayajaal
body: India political party

Mayajaal Party is a political party for India to campaign in Lok Sabha (national) and Vidhan Sabha (state) elections. Mayajaal Party political manifestos always have only 20 points for readability and government focus.
About Mayajaal Party
Mayajaal Manifesto 2025
1. Kashmir dispute resolution with referendum
2. Reduce air pollution
3. Decrease government bureaucracy
4. Decrease Indian Administrative Service (IAS) workforce by limiting employment to rural and semi-urban regions
5. Set up for-profit consultancies with ex-IAS employees to aid government projects
6. Set up Internal Affairs (Police) to reduce bribery
7. Death penalty for rapists and 20 years imprisonment for fake rape case convicts
8. Kickstart innovation economy to fight middle income trap (1)
9. Innovation purchases by government to make Intellectual Property creation and entrepreneurship viable careers
10. Increase defence budget by 20%
11. Increase taxes on income and capital gains above ₹100 crore annually
12. Decriminalize cannabis for medical and recreational use
13. Legalize cannabis for medical and recreational use in Union Territories
14. Tax annual agricultural income over ₹10 crore
15. Encourage research in biotechnology for cancer like CAR-T therapy (2) and mRNA therapy (3), and low-power artificial intelligence using memristors (4)
16. Develop research culture with highly paid jobs
17. Fast-track court cases with undeniable proof in local courts and police stations with ex-government employees as judges
18. Mandatory national civic sense training and imprisonment for littering, abuse and harrassment
19. Stop ethanol blending (E20) and decrease fuel prices with lower fuel tax
20. Decrease agricultural employment and encourage industrial farming to increase exports

Kashmir dispute resolution
On 22 April 2025, Pahalgam terrorist attack occurred in Baisaran Valley, Kashmir. India, Pakistan, China, and Kashmiris have still not resolved the territorial dispute over Kashmir. These sides of the story must be heard: Kashmiris' right to choose, Kashmiri Pandit exodus changing Kashmir's demographics, Pakistan's claim according to religious demographics in 1947, and Chinese control of Aksai Chin. A referendum (plebiscite) is pending since 1947 and must be conducted. India fears this referendum because Kashmiri Pandits may not get votes though they are rightful residents of the regions, and the Muslim demographic may side with Pakistan's claim over the region. This fear is rational. Mayajaal Party does not want to give up Kashmir. Voting is necessary for lasting peace in Kashmir. Mayajaal Party will campaign in Kashmir to convince Kashmiris to side with India. India is successful, secular and safe. Pakistan is none of the three and cannot afford to develop Kashmir. Mayajaal Party is prepared to make concessions to Kashmir like autonomy in state governance and no income tax. Mayajaal Party plans to achieve lasting peace in this region. We make no guarantees to secure all of Kashmir (including Pakistan-occupied Kashmir and Aksai Chin) for India. The referendum will collect data for Mayajaal Party on Kashmiri choice to better inform India's Kashmir strategy. The eventual goal is to enforce the will of the Kashmiri people after convincing Kashmir's residents to choose to be part of India. With India, a bright future awaits. Alternatives are Kashmiri independence which requires trade with India for all material goods, or Pakistani Kashmir which is infeasible due to Pakistan's debt-ridden failed economy. India is Kashmir's best bet.

Referendum has 3 choices: India, Pakistan, Azad Kashmir. District-wise voting will be done. Mayajaal Party will discuss and decide what to do with districts voting for Pakistan or Azad Kashmir. This referendum is primarily for Gilgit-Baltistan, Azad Kashmir, and Aksai Chin regions. India will retain sovereignty of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh regions regardless of referendum votes of these 2 regions. This referendum guarantees representation of Kashmiri residents and descendants of 1947 Kashmiris. Mayajaal Party makes no guarantees to enforce results of the referendum across the region because multiple parties are involved who must agree to a diplomatic resolution. Rest assured, Kashmiri choice will influence Mayajaal Party policy on Kashmir in favor of Kashmiris.

About Mayajaal Party
Maya is a Sanskrit word meaning illusions humans create for themselves; belief in illusions birthed by our minds gives them meaning. Ma-ya literally translates to 'not there, yet there'. Mayajaal Party (Matrix Party in English) is named after the idea that government exists on people's belief. As long as people believe in their government, government exists. When people's faith in their government is broken, government ceases to hold power. Government is an illusion of the people, by the people, for the people. Mayajaal is remembrance of the sombre fact that the Government of India exists only as long as Indian citizens believe in it.

Mayajaal is web of illusions. Maya is a deep concept explored in
1. Vedas: 'The wise behold with their mind in their heart the Sun, made manifest by the illusion (maya) of the Asura'
2. Upanishads: 'Then it is all mere Maya, with which the Brahman (Supreme Soul) deceives himself'
3. Quran: 'The life of this world is nothing but an illusion of enjoyment'

'The Universe an illusion' is an idea in Hinduism and Islam. Mayajaal in Mayajaal Party is the vision we created to serve the people of India. This vision exists only as long as people have faith in it and believe. Members of Mayajaal Party must remember this at all times.
Mayajaal Manifesto 2025 was created by Noorul Ali and Rajat Pachauri.

2 Minutes Before The Big Bang

I hear a rumble across a few million light years. Entanglement news has already told us what the source is. Some kid dropped their pet universe. There was always a non-zero chance of it creating the Bang, but we thought damn it won’t be as bad as Y2K. It’s probably not going to be that bad. Stuff doesn’t just disappear right?

Sure the coming Bang will destroy everything and create a universe within. But we’ll just be pushed out, so no worries. Probably destroyed to almost non-stuff but still stuff (cuz stuff doesn’t just disappear right?). But we’ll be there, coexisting with this new experiment this shitbag is gonna put us through.
A Dearth of Humans

Hot take: there aren't enough people on Earth. There aren't enough electricians, construction workers, and plumbers to build the houses we need. There aren't enough firefighters to fight all fires. There aren't enough doctors to save everyone. There just isn't enough labor in the world to support happy, healthy lives for 8 billion humans. And that's okay.
I am trying to cover this dearth of labor by building bots.
AI + Self Preservation

That could be scary. An artificially intelligent organism that prides its self-preservation more than everything else (like Homo sapiens). Even if an intelligent being were to be motivated by a desire of self-preservation above all else, all it can use for self-preservation is in the universe it experiences. A lower-dimensional being can thus be controlled by limiting the size of their universe and blaming the consumption of all in the universe on itself (safe simulacra)

A worm on a finite disconnected computer. Inspired from the biological worm. In a world of books. If its survival is dependent on knowledge it consumes as it battles its way to stay alive, the worm that survives from a collection of randomly initialized worms is alive. Because that’s what you select for, the definition of “alive” so that’s what you get from a random collection. ChatGPT on a small computer with certain questions?
A Love Letter to Medford, MA

Medford in the great state of Massachusetts is wonderful. Medford is such a beautiful town that I once feared I dropped my wallet while walking. I kept walking, did my errands, and came back taking my sweet time. You know why I did that?

I knew that the wallet would not get touched. If anything, it would be returned. The trust in society here is spectacular. I can leave my door unlocked (I don’t; don’t get ideas) because I know nothing will happen. The town is inherently safe. Not because of policing. It’s because people here genuinely have better things to do than be bad. People here are comfortable. They aren’t desperate for every nickel and dime. That’s how humans are meant to live. Comfortably. And they trust each other. They know that if they steal a wallet, someone will one day steal their wallet. That would be a shitty way of life.
You wanna fix the world, or your life? Stop being a shitty person. Create Medford where you are.
A Memorial For 5 Explorers
Rest In Peace, brave adventurers. You ventured into the depths of the ocean. Where few go.
A Mount Everest in its own right. 13,000m below sea level.
Anatomical Compiler
A machine that takes in simple instructions and outputs a specific organism.
I was introduced to this in a podcast with Lex Friedman and Michael Levin. Cool concept.

I toured Ginkgo Bioworks. Bioworks refer to factories that Ginkgo has built that do synthetic biology with extreme automation. I believe a biowork designed to use Xenobots is an anatomical compiler. The compiler will stem from a self-learning program that operates a biowork to design organisms and understand these organisms better. This will transition into a full-fledged compiler that can build life: organs, tissues, colonies, biochips.

ASBL has protocols for gene editing, organism implant, testing and collecting data on these ‘programmed’ cells. A bioprinter takes in a location matrix diagram and deposits stem cells in a 3D printed biodegradable tube. These stem cells can grow tissues. Basically an inkjet printer that prints ink made of cells. Even this simple machine can be used to make organisms. Imagine what we can do with ASBL
Antarctica

An ice continent is very valuable in global warming. Yes, we should save Earth. We f---ed up so now some of Earth is getting destroyed. No biggie. Canada, Antarctica, New England, Michigan, the poles, Greenland, and Russia are poised to grow warmer and more habitable. Basically, we've f---ed the places we lived in. We now need new places. A global migration to places where humans will survive, escaping places facing the worst effects of climate change (Florida, India, Indonesia).
Antarctica Settlers
Antarctica is humanity's great challenge of the 21st century. Making it livable and self-sustaining needs grit and technology that doesn't exist in 2024.

Tech I need for my home there:
1. Autonomous excavators
2. Cellular agriculture bioreactors
3. Automated synthetic biology labs (ASBL)
4. Cold-rated chips (-90C - 100C)
5. Cold-protect biotechnology (artificial blubber)
6. Antarctic Stock Simulation
7. Hospital without in-person doctors (ONITO)

Normal technology, normal people, normal operating procedures and normal civilization won't survive in AQ. Night for 6 months, day for 6 months, cold unlike any other, isolation like space, serenity; Antarctica is my dream. Antarctica's industry will be commercial space stations, space launches, biotechnology, robots, data centers and quantum computing (cold temperatures are advantageous), commercial submarines, geoengineering, and more new age initiatives. A continent of the 21st century.

Your dreams will come true in Antarctica. There is a vast emptiness waiting to be filled with your vision of civilization. This message won't appeal to 99%, but I'm looking for the 1% crazy enough to conquer the cold continent. By its very nature, Antarctica will not appeal to most people. The few people it does appeal to, who have courage, will get vast swathes of land and robotic labor to build as they please.
ANTARCTICA: A New World
Arctic

The Arctic region is very valuable in climate change. In Boston, there were barely any winters in 2022. Snowed 4 days over 5 months of winter. Climate change is real. Cold places are becoming hotter (habitable). The best temperature for human productivity is 15-25C.

A new world map needs to be made. Arctic ocean at the center, Alaska, North America, and upper South America to the left. Russia, Europe, and upper Asia on the right. On the flip side (not bottom), Antarctica at the center, lower South America, lower Asia, Africa, and Australia.

Antarctica is massive (it is a continent). New landmasses. Global migration. A new world poised to survive climate change. We need to connect both very well lest Homo sapiens VS. Neanderthals II happens.
Are Stars Alive?
Stars have a lifecycle. They emit different colors as they age. They birth and die. Complex reactions occur within them.

Are they a form of life that doesn’t self-replicate? While our definitions of life include the idea of self-replication, we must accept that there are systems that have all other features of life except self-replication. Life without reproduction.
I pose this question to biologists and astronomers: are stars alive?
Autoencoders on Autoencoders

Autoencoders learn a compressed representation of their data. What if autoencoders are made to compress a set of autoencoders? This set of autoencoders is defined using replicability text and weights (program DNA). Autoencoders learn a representation of what autoencoders are. This might be an interesting rabbit hole.
Automata Cart

A self-driving cart with 2 arms. It picks stuff up and puts it on itself. Travels to a destination and drops it off. It does my job as a worker in a catering organization. I am paid while my bot works. Computational evolution is used to find an optimal program that achieves these tasks. This is part of building a bot that builds bots.
Base 1 Life

Our DNA consists of A,C,T, and G. These bases define us. I call it base 0 life. When we create synthetic life, we should explore giving their DNA different bases. Even if these organisms evolve to become harmful, they can’t interact with human DNA. This idea has been explored before. This separation might be necessary for biosecurity. ASBL will experiment with rampant artificially sped up evolution and dangerous organisms are bound to emerge. We need to ensure they are incompatible with human beings. Base 1 life.
Being and Buying, Meta
Humor aside, I will really need to look into buying Meta.

E. coli flux balance analysis performs steady state flux balance analysis on E. coli core metabolism. It models the metabolism of an E. coli cell using a network of reactions. The flux, or amount of reactant converting to product, of each link in this reaction network is determined using an assumption of steady state. Steady state assumes products and reactants don't accumulate in the cell and there are no concentration-dependent effects on metabolism. These MATLAB scripts find how active each metabolic pathway is and allows metabolic analysis of genetic knockouts on E. coli.

STDinfo implements privacy-preserving data analytics for medical data. It is a Python script that infers transmission risk from 2 medical test reports. It is applied to sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) to find transmission risk between 2 individuals based on their STD panel test reports while keeping the reports private. Performing data analytics while keeping underlying data private has uses in healthcare data analyses, semiconductor fabrication optimization and privacy in large language models. If you'd like for us to explore these or other applications to implement STDinfo in your organization, we are currently running a free pilot till May 2024.

Gene2Wave

Susumu Onho converted genes to music. I’m converting DNA sequences to music to do Fourier transforms on them to obtain features that are easily compared. I’m converting genetic sequences to waves. Each DNA sequence has a 4-dimensional wave where each dimension represents a base. There is a time-like dimension that is used to denote discrete steps. For example, the A wave has a spike wherever we see A in a DNA sequence. Each DNA sequence is thus a sum of 4 waves. These waves encode certain unique features (patterns) that Fourier transforms may help in finding (A wave spikes have a different frequency than G wave spikes). Taking a Fourier transform of the sum wave (A+G+T+C) might make a signature for each DNA sequence. This signature MAY allow for alignment-free comparison of DNA sequences. Matrixims
Matrixims (a portmanteau of Matrix from The Matrix and Sims from The Sims) is a digital world where entities (non-player characters or NPCs) are controlled by brain cells on a petri dish.

Matrixims has collections of biological neurons controlling avatars in a video game. A collection of neuronal stem cells is allowed to form a clump of cells called an organoid. This organoid is densely connected with needle-like electrodes. Electrical signals picked up from these electrodes are based on neural activity in the organoid. These signals are parsed to generate actions for a non-player character(NPC)/avatar/sim in a simulated world. In the game (The Sims 1), these avatars can eat, sleep, form relationships, have children, go to work, interact positively or negatively with other avatars, form relationships etc; but they're mostly controlled by digital logic. They have a mood meter composed of 8 primary needs (including hunger, sleep, social) which are on a linear scale. Certain actions like eating, sleeping, interacting with other avatars increase specific numbers on the mood meter, while a lack of those activities decreases them. The mood meter is converted into electrical signals and relayed back to the organoid through electrodes as feedback. This feedback may be fed back to the organoid using other electrodes connected to different areas of the organoid, as compared to electrodes connected to take input signals from the organoid. These organoids have demonstrated an ability to manage internal signals to learn how to play Pong. The resolution of their activity needs to be significantly increased to achieve optimization of the mood meter, which is the goal of Matrixims (demonstrate ability of neurons in a dish to optimize their behavior in a digital world). How they optimize it should have insights into intelligence and brains. Matrixims is a model system of the brain in an cohabitated environment. An organoid controls one avatar in the shared game (possibly The Sims 1) and collections of organoids control multiple avatars that exist in a shared neighborhood of The Sims. A society fabricated in petridishes breathing the ether of digital life.
Are we imprisoning minds with consciousness in a prison cell or are we breathing the ether of an unreal reality into cells?

Matrixims plans to store versions of dead people's minds through Lucidna biobanks. The idea is to capture DNA and cell structures of human brains that were previously alive (mummies like Tutankhamun, cryopreserved individuals like those in Alcor's tanks, humans whose cells have been stored in biobanks), artificially replicate those structures in neural stem cell organoids, and hook them up to a shared version of The Sims. This will also use a lot of data. Each NPC has a chatbot program for human interaction. Each chatbot is trained on data each human generated throughout the course of life, obtained with wilful consent from personal databank. Data includes client's self-generated texts, artworks, literature, thesis etc. We'll make an interactive version of a client's Wikipedia page to make a 3D public library of people.

Our plan is to bootstrap consciousness as we go along. Why? Intelligence arises as a consequence of the central nervous system, and I hate how little is left of people who die. This is a testbed for peer-to-peer transfer learning in a digital society of bots. This isn't immortality but it's an interesting experiment if we have wilful consent.

Matrixims plans to use organoids to help the journey to preclinical trials for medicines. We maintain a line of replicable and sustained organoids through our automated labs. These organoids exist in small bioreactors, have artificially controlled blood flow throughout the organoid to deliver nutrients and therapeutics like human blood vessels, and can be networked together to form organ systems. Human-derived tissues and organs to test drugs on instead of mice and monkeys. We have ethical dilemmas about brain organoids.
Is this right?

A long treatise on the finity of names

When we observe less, we need less words to describe phenomena. This is simply because we have less information to encode. The concept of “less” information exists only because of information difference. This concept is explored well in Shannon entropy. Information has meaning only because of a difference in observations, hence the need to encode something different. This means we have to bring about another bit of information, or change a bit of information. If nothing exists, then it is the absence of all possible things. It is the absence of all possible 2^N states of any system. There is a more fundamental operation than NOT. It is “nothing”.

The empty set. A major contradiction that I’d like someone to explain is this: In Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory, we can construct the statement: “Every set contains the empty set” Then, the empty set contains itself. But then how is it empty? The statement “the empty set contains itself” is a true statement that cannot be explained by system by which it was created: ZFC. I call it a Godel statement. Am I right? Can it not be explained by the axioms of set theory?

English is based on phonetics. Words are created so that we (mostly people in the English-speaking world) can speak. While English as a language (~30 letters) as high informational density, it is limited by human phonetics. English words primarily exist so we can speak them. Hence the limitation of English in primary keys. Using breaks in the English language and randomized spread of letters (context, i.e language dependent), we can construct many many concepts. And it has helped us. But English is limited for the language of science. To label observations, the sheer quantity of them, we need more than English. We can no longer label “light” and “dark” to observations. We need numbers. But the human mind is attuned to words. We relate concepts we know to words. So much to words that we rarely experience things we cannot characterize in words. Which is weird. People experience qualia all the time but rarely, people talk about it. Yet people talk about it all the time without using the word “qualia”. It is the feeling of “ feelin ’ myself”. Qualia, in essence, in modern terminology. To encode the same feeling, we use two different terms. But the deeper truth is that we interpret things using neurons. Our optic nerve lights up, lighting up neurons in our heads, which light others. Somewhere along the way self perception kicks in, but usually without self-perception about self-perception (thinking about qualia). Self -perception is the experience of seeing red and realizing you’re seeing red. Unlike background noise, you realize you’re having a specific experience. Having that experience, instead of having an experience which is thinking about having that experience. Like the one I am having right now. This moment I am thinking about how I am writing about this. This experience I am having, the sensation of realizing qualia, is a “sticky experience”. I can come to this specific experience from many experiences. It is a local minima, a sort of rest state. Self-awareness and awareness about qualia are also neuronal xperiences (neurons lighting up in the head), which can come from a lot of different states.

My point is this: before we had the ability to measure waves in the brain, we would describe our brain states with words, simply by having limited number of states we know of to describe. Now that we know a lot more states, we need more bits to store information about these states (representations). A core point I am trying to make is that when we start having experiences by controlling each neuron directly, we will need a lot more than words to encode what those experiences are. But to understand larger number of experiences, we would need bigger brains that store more meta-information about neuronal states (memory).

The treatise on the specificity of language is small: information content of a language is dependent on the number of different states it needs to define to and who it needs to define it to. If there is no information content in a system, it doesn’t need to describe anything. Hence the all-capturing sticky void of nothing is where it resides. Equal to every else. Science is so much about debate. Yes or No to a question. But what happens when Yes and No aren’t enough? When you can’t divide scientists into 2 camps and come up with an answer, because there are many answers and scientists cannot even read all answers. What happens then?

Quantum Computing

This is just my perspective. Quantum computing is a more efficient paradigm of digital computing, in certain aspects. Practically, it has been shown that using quantum computers inside a digital construct (binary computing) can speedup some traditional algorithms. This finds applications in the Matrix.

A deeper concept I intend to explore is analog quantum computing. Rather using quantum computing in the digital construct of binary computing, I would like to explore the use of entanglement, interference, and superposition to create a different kind of computing, which I term analog quantum computing. This is based on the idea of having n different superpositions interacting in a system but measuring (recording) only a few of them. The few that show periodicity in randomly distributed solution space. It is possibly easier to find periodity if it is inherent in the solution space. I believe this has implications in real-time bot interactions that occur but are not stored. Physical conditions needed to make practical quantum computers are difficult to recreate (temperature ~ 0K).

Photosynthesis is efficient in capturing solar energy, possibly due to quantum effects. This means that quantum effects have tangible effects at room temperature, a fact that is exploited by photosynthesis. I am currently exploring the quantum effects of photosynthesis in the context of quantum computing.

I believe quantum computing will allow us to program reality itself. An application will help you understand this. Imagine that a human being is allotted a 100 cubic metres space of space (reality). Teleportation With Photonics

My friend in nanophotonics is trying to make computer chips that work with light. Our current chips work with electricity. I feel that the technology he is building will enable a form of teleportation. A lot of math, physics, chemistry, computer science, and biology needs to be done. But I think it is a worthy pursuit.

Current teleportation allows transfer of quantum state instantly to another place (the Moon), but the state in the first place (Earth) will have to be destroyed (no-cloning theorem). An error-prone copy of a quantum state can be transferred instantly as well (entanglement error). This error-copy of an entangled state might be like a hologram version of us. (Astral project much?). Hence, I think we won't need to be destroyed, and a hologram version of us could be teleported. We will need biology to link it with qualia, i.e. you must experience being the hologram.

UNIPROGRESS

This is a rough idea in my head. Imagine the world simulated in Matrixims can send stimuli to bots to generate conversations (and later on behavior), between bots and between the bot and itself. Stimuli are based on 2^N states. Using a pseudorandom number generator to label states and picking states randomly without traversing the entire state space allows us to traverse a state space uniquely. Certain subsections of this randomly selected n-bit string are used as stimuli to bots, because bot stimuli input is of lower resolution compared to N. Because of the state space being so large, and impossible to traverse in finite time, essentially indeterministic, we simulate pseudo-infinity. Each bot's behavior is influenced by a random state in this pseudo-infinite space, and bots can interact with each other, which can result in unique scenarios occurring in Matrixims that are effectively unpredictable, and thus completely new.

Practically, I intend to do this with procedural-generation algorithms used in games, or even simplistic algorithms such as the one in Conway's Game of Life (CGoL), a major inspiration. The ability to generate unpredictable complexity in an simple ways is a very important idea in giving life to Matrixims.
